note: Email-only leads. Priority scale: 8-10 = high, 5-7 = medium, 1-4 = low. Phone included at 80%+ confidence; blank if not found.
deduplication_note: Orange Frazer Press and Porkbelly Press were present in the 2026-03-26 run and excluded from this run. Belt Publishing, Two Dollar Radio, Darcy Pattison / Mims House, and Middle West Press excluded — no verified email found.

leads.name: New Michigan Press
leads.notes: Ann Arbor-based chapbook press — same city as Cushing-Malloy — publishing broadsides, anthologies, and chapbooks since 1999 in partnership with DIAGRAM magazine. The 2026 Chapbook Contest closes April 30, 2026, meaning a winner goes to print this summer. Outreach angle: local same-city turnaround, no shipping delays, one dedicated contact from proof to delivery.

leads.name: Glass Poetry Press
leads.notes: Toledo, Ohio micro-press publishing 4–6 chapbooks per year in runs of ~100 copies each, hand-bound with full-color card stock covers and ISBN. Annual open reading period closes October 31 — winner notification and production follows shortly after. Outreach angle: quality short-run offset/digital for covers and text blocks, 20-day turnaround to meet post-contest production windows.

leads.name: Ice Cube Press
leads.notes: Iowa-based independent Midwest press founded 1991, publishing nonfiction, fiction, poetry, and graphic novels; 2023 Iowa Governor's Art Award and perennial 'Best Indie Press in Iowa.' Maintains two imprints and multiple annual releases. Verified direct contact (steve@icecubepress.com) makes outreach straightforward. Outreach angle: Midwest-made production, regional pride, and a single point of contact they'll actually reach.

leads.name: 42 Miles Press
leads.notes: University press at Indiana University South Bend publishing poetry books and chapbooks; 2025 contest winner Steve Castro recently selected, meaning a new title enters production now. Just 2 hours from Ann Arbor — same-day proofing visits are feasible. Annual prize cycle creates predictable, repeatable printing demand. Outreach angle: fast-turn proofs and author-friendly communication.

leads.name: Midwest Writing Center (MWC Press)
leads.notes: Rock Island, IL nonprofit press publishing the Foster-Stahl Chapbook Series for upper Midwest writers (IL, IN, IA, MI, MN, OH, WI). Submissions open through March 2026 — a winner is being selected now for production. Phone and email both verified. Outreach angle: Cushing-Malloy's Midwest roots and 75+ years of book manufacturing make them the natural regional printing partner.

leads.source: Poets & Writers Small Presses DB / IBPA Directory
leads.name: Yellow Arrow Publishing
leads.notes: Baltimore-based nonprofit hybrid publisher focused on women-identifying writers, releasing 3 chapbooks per year on a fixed schedule (April, July, October). 2025 authors already announced; 2026 selection cycle is underway. Known for quality design and professional presentation. Outreach angle: consistent repeat production relationship with a press that takes physical book quality seriously.
